_comment: Event template — copy into content/events/<id>.json — the file name equals the entity id (validator warns on mismatch). Keys starting with _ are authoring comments; the validator strips them. Run npm run content:validate before opening a PR.
_id: kebab-case ascii slug, unique across ALL entity types, e.g. "war-of-independence". Include a year in the id only to disambiguate (e.g. "arab-revolt-1936"); hierarchy is expressed by parentId, never by filename.
_title: Short Hebrew title, fits on a timeline card.
_description: 1-3 Hebrew sentences for the detail panel.

description.he: תיאור קצר של האירוע, משפט אחד עד שלושה.

_dates: start/end use "YYYY" | "YYYY-MM" | "YYYY-MM-DD" (Gregorian). Omit end for a point-in-time event. "approx": true marks a circa date. Never invent precision you don't have — a year-only date is displayed as a year.
_parentId: Optional: id of the parent event when this is a sub-event (e.g. a battle inside a war). Add the key only when needed.
_importance: Integer 1-100 per the docs/spec/zoom.md rubric: 90-100 era-defining (visible fully zoomed out); 70-89 major national events; 40-69 notable events and sub-events; 20-39 contextual detail (year-level zoom); 1-19 fine detail (month-level zoom). A sub-event must score LOWER than its parent (validator warns otherwise).
_regionIds: ids from content/taxonomies/regions.json; filtering by a parent region includes all its descendants.
_tags: Optional free-form strings (not validated against a taxonomy). Add a "tags": [] key only when needed.
_image: Optional: { "src": "...", "alt": { "he": "..." }, "credit": "..." }. Prefer a Wikimedia Commons direct file URL (upload.wikimedia.org/...) — stable and hotlink-safe — with a credit naming the source/license.
_video: Optional and rare — only when real, good-quality archival footage exists from a reputable channel: { "provider": "youtube", "videoId": "11-char-id", "title": { "he": "..." }, "credit": "..." }. Rendered via a fixed youtube-nocookie.com embed (decision D19) — never a raw URL or embed HTML.
_sources: REQUIRED — at least one citation backing the facts (docs/spec/content.md#sourcing). Every source needs a real, stable "url" (decision D18) — a source with no link doesn't help the reader, so unlinked citations are rejected. Prefer an authoritative institution (national library, archive, university, museum) or a Hebrew-Wikipedia article. "publisher" and "kind" (archive|library|museum|encyclopedia|reference|academic|government|book|press|website) are optional. Placeholder URLs (containing "...", example.com) and non-http(s) URLs are rejected.
